mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier discounting and liquidity conditions into equities, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next month when policy is actively easing.
failure_condition: If 20-day SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.03, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation is still elevated, real-rate pressure and tighter financial conditions suppress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of cases, or if the average forward return in the triggered state is not below the unconditional average by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income expectations and recession risk, which leads equity investors to discount lower future earnings and pushes S&P 500 returns lower over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of episodes after a >0.3pp 3-month rise in UNRATE, or if the signal's rolling 5-year hit rate falls below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid PAYEMS expansion together with stable or falling UNRATE reflects broad labor-market resilience that supports consumption and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change > 0 and UNRATE is flat or lower versus 3 months ago
failure_condition: Reject if triggered periods do not deliver positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the average return under the trigger is not above the unconditional mean by at least 0.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal consumer spending and revenue growth, which improves forward earnings expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the 3-month growth rate in RSAFS has no positive association with 20-day SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window, or if triggered high-growth periods do not outperform baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in INDPRO after prior weakness signals easing supply constraints and improving industrial activity, which should support equity returns over the next quarter through better profit expectations.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change turns positive after a prior 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if post-rebound episodes in INDPRO fail to produce positive 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the average forward return is not above zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising HOUST combined with a stable labor market indicates durable housing demand and low recession risk, which supports cyclical earnings and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not deliver positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of occurrences, or if the signal weakens to a non-positive average return across a rolling 4-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO is weakening while CPIAUCSL inflation remains sticky, margins are squeezed by stagflationary pressure and S&P 500 forward returns turn negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of cases, or if the average triggered return exceeds the unconditional mean.
